scene_prompt: A robed scribe-monk at a wooden writing desk in a candlelit scriptorium, beginning a new illuminated page with the first large gilded initial letter of a love poem, quill in hand, fresh creamy parchment unrolled, eight blank card-shaped frames sketched faintly in silverpoint on the page beside the letter, a tiny stack of tarot cards and a small wooden abacus sitting on the desk corner

A robed scribe spreading all eight illuminated tarot Cards of Virtue across his desk in a careful fan, while a second hooded figure beside him sings into a small bronze speaking-horn with golden notes of song drifting upward; behind them a wooden rack holds twenty-eight tiny rolled scrolls, each labelled with a paired pair of virtue-symbols

A bewildered pilgrim with a parchment road-map stands at a forked path inside a stone archway carved with the word CARDS; a friendly cartographer-monk leans out and erects a small hand-painted signpost reading THIS WAY, smoothing a tangle in the inked roadway with his thumb; a tiny scroll-carrying messenger bird arrows toward the corrected path

Two stone alcoves side by side — one labelled READING, one labelled DECK — being merged by a craftsman-mason carefully removing a single dividing wall stone; on the back wall of the now-unified chamber, a great tapestry showing all eight virtue cards in a single row; a scribe at the floor writes a small parchment labelled CULTIVATE THIS VIRTUE

A medieval clockmaker reaching deep into a great brass mechanism behind a hanging tapestry, gently untangling a single golden gear that had jammed; through a small window beside him, a row of tiny tarot cards on a distant wagon table glow alive again with renewed candlelight; a scroll labelled CARDS lies open on the floor with its painted roadway arrows now pointing correctly

A patient illuminator measuring the margins of a manuscript page with a wooden compass, leaving a generous strip along the bottom where eight tiny miniature tarot cards rest in a tidy row; to the side, a small arrow-shaped bookmark labelled SKIP AHEAD sticks out from between the pages, painted in vermillion and gold

A scribe-monk hanging a freshly-painted gold-edged signboard outside the wagon-fair entrance, the signboard depicting a tiny row of the eight tarot cards, an arrow with two paths labelled BEGIN and SKIP AHEAD, and a small banner across the top reading README; a second monk presses a circular wax seal onto the bottom corner of the sign
